title: [InstCombine] Fold (add (add A, 1), (sext (icmp ne A, 0))) to call umax(A, 1)

tests.source_program:
define i64 @i64_type_variation(i64 %A) {
  %add1 = add i64 %A, 1
  %icmp = icmp ne i64 %A, 0
  %sext = sext i1 %icmp to i64
  %add2 = add i64 %add1, %sext
  ret i64 %add2
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary does not mention support for different integer types (i64, i16, i8). The patch works for any integer type but the summary only discusses the pattern structure without addressing type variations.

alive2.src:
define i64 @i64_type_variation(i64 %A) {
  %add1 = add i64 %A, 1
  %icmp = icmp ne i64 %A, 0
  %sext = sext i1 %icmp to i64
  %add2 = add i64 %add1, %sext
  ret i64 %add2
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i64 @i64_type_variation(i64 %A) {
#0:
  %add1 = add i64 %A, 1
  %icmp = icmp ne i64 %A, 0
  %sext = sext i1 %icmp to i64
  %add2 = add i64 %add1, %sext
  ret i64 %add2
}
=>
define i64 @i64_type_variation(i64 %A) {
#0:
  %add2 = umax i64 %A, 1
  ret i64 %add2
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.source_program:
define i32 @A_has_multiple_uses(i32 %A) {
  %other_use = add i32 %A, 100
  %add1 = add i32 %A, 1
  %icmp = icmp ne i32 %A, 0
  %sext = sext i1 %icmp to i32
  %add2 = add i32 %add1, %sext
  ret i32 %add2
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary emphasizes one-use constraints on intermediate values (add1, icmp, sext) but does not clarify that the base value A itself can have multiple uses elsewhere in the function.

alive2.src:
define i32 @A_has_multiple_uses(i32 %A) {
  %other_use = add i32 %A, 100
  %add1 = add i32 %A, 1
  %icmp = icmp ne i32 %A, 0
  %sext = sext i1 %icmp to i32
  %add2 = add i32 %add1, %sext
  ret i32 %add2
}
